content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agents to treat failed exploration trajectories as a self-supervised 'intrinsic curiosity curriculum' rather than discarding them: failed trajectories that do not yield expected state transitions are weighted as higher-curiosity signals to update the agent's active memory, rather than relying solely on novelty of unseen states. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where the agent's own failures continuously refine its exploration bias without needing external reward labels.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
